Uploading to Storage

Titles can upload items to storage for various reasons. One of the most common is to upload game clips. When uploading game clips, use the XOnlineStorageUpload function. Note that your title must first call XOnlineStatWrite and XOnlineStatWriteGetResult. For details, see Uploading Game Clips.

To upload data for other purposes, use the XOnlineStorageUploadByServerPath or XOnlineStorageUploadFromMemory function. Both of these functions require a server path, which your title can build using the XOnlineStorageCreateServerPath function. Server paths are composed of a service facility name, a user ID (where applicable), a team ID (where applicable), and the file name.

XOnlineStorageUploadByServerPath uploads from a a file on the disk. Note that data stored on the hard disk requires saved game or content signatures to be uploaded.

XOnlineStorageUploadFromMemory uploads from a memory buffer. When it performs the upload, it also attaches the necessary Xbox Live signatures to the data automatically. No further signatures are required.
